questions.question: Which command moves Karel forward by exactly one corner in the direction it is facing?
questions.explanation: move() advances Karel one corner; the others rotate or manipulate beepers.

questions.question: What happens if Karel executes move() when there is a wall directly ahead?
options: A) Karel climbs over the wall
B) Karel crashes/throws an error and stops
C) Karel waits until the wall disappears
D) Karel removes the wall and moves
questions.explanation: Moving into a wall causes a runtime error; guard moves with frontIsClear().

questions.question: Which condition checks whether a beeper is on Karel’s current corner?
questions.explanation: beepersPresent() tests beepers at the current corner; beepersInBag() is about Karel’s inventory.

questions.question: Choose the best description of while vs. if in Karel programs.
options: A) if runs repeatedly; while runs once
B) Both run repeatedly as long as the condition is true
C) if runs at most once; while repeats while the condition is true
questions.explanation: An if executes the body once at most; a while re-evaluates the condition and may run many times.

questions.question: In base Karel (not SuperKarel), which helper correctly defines turnRight()?
questions.explanation: Three left turns equal one right turn.

questions.question: Consider: while (frontIsClear()) { putBeeper(); move(); } starting on the first corner of a row. Which is true?
options: A) The last corner will be left without a beeper (fencepost bug)
B) The first corner is skipped
C) Two beepers end up on the last corner
D) It places a beeper on every corner correctly
questions.explanation: The loop stops before the last corner because frontIsClear() becomes false; the last corner never gets a beeper.

questions.question: Which loop moves Karel from its current corner straight to the wall ahead (without crashing)?
questions.explanation: Repeatedly moving while the path is clear reaches the wall safely.

questions.question: Which statement about SuperKarel is true?
options: A) Base Karel already has turnRight()
B) SuperKarel adds helpers like turnRight() and turnAround()
C) putBeeper() exists only in SuperKarel
questions.explanation: SuperKarel extends Karel with convenience methods such as turnRight() and turnAround().

questions.question: A good postcondition for a helper method moveToWall() is:
options: A) Karel is at the origin
C) Karel is facing the same direction with a wall immediately ahead
D) Karel has placed a beeper
questions.explanation: moveToWall() typically preserves direction and stops with frontIsBlocked().

questions.question: Given: if (frontIsClear()) { move(); } else { turnLeft(); } and a wall is ahead, what happens?
options: B) Karel moves through the wall
questions.explanation: The condition is false, so the else branch executes and Karel turns left.
